mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, predicting negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer price inflation indicates stagflationary pressure, compressing equity multiples and driving negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% acts as a leading indicator for broader demand contraction, forecasting negative 20-day forward returns as credit conditions tighten.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines during rate hikes do not produce statistically significant negative 20-day forward returns (t-stat < -1.645), or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 reinforces corporate earnings growth expectations, generating positive 5-day forward returns through earnings revision momentum.
failure_condition: Payrolls growth above 150k consistently coincides with flat or negative 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the break-even inflation rate implied by CPI trends, real rates turn sharply positive, triggering negative 60-day forward returns as discount rates outpace nominal growth.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered periods, or forward returns in high real-rate regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial output growth, a sudden deceleration in consumer credit growth signals a credit crunch that will constrain future demand, predicting negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Credit deceleration during high industrial output does not lead to negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% annualized rate sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive 60-day forward returns as earnings momentum translates to equity valuation support.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all test periods.
